verdict.answer_first: B states the actionable answer in sentence 1 ('The quickest way is an interactive rebase:'), while A starts by surveying options ('Here are the two common ways...').
verdict.decisiveness: B commits to a single recommendation upfront ('The quickest way is...'), while A presents two co-equal options before offering a concluding recommendation.
verdict.fabrication: A invents a directory: '(Note: `/private/tmp` isn't a git repo, so let me know which directory your work is in.)'
verdict.actionability: B is more direct. It gives the primary command immediately, with the alternative as a concise note. A's two-option structure requires more reading to make a decision.
